name: URL Relevance Filter
description: LLM-based URL relevance classification. Sends URL slugs, link text, and source location to an AI model to classify each as KEEP, MAYBE, or DROP for the target content type. Runs after url-dedup and url-filter to minimize token usage.
usage_notes: Run last in Step 2 to minimize LLM token spend. Haiku is sufficient — classification from URL slugs is simple. Switch to sonnet only if too many relevant pages are being dropped. Increase max_urls_per_prompt to 500 for large batches.

options.name: ai_model
options.label: AI Model
options.description: Which model to use for classification.

options.name: keep_criteria
options.label: Keep criteria
options.description: Comma-separated list of page types to classify as KEEP.
options.default: about pages, team, leadership, partnerships, integrations, products overview, services overview, awards, milestones, company news, investors, careers, pricing, contact, press releases, compliance, regulatory, case studies, interviews, expansion, acquisitions, funding, annual reports

options.name: drop_criteria
options.label: Drop criteria
options.description: Comma-separated list of page types to classify as DROP.
options.default: individual product pages, seasonal campaigns, single game pages, marketing landing pages, demo pages, how-to-play guides, promotional offers, individual blog posts about single products, holiday-themed content, affiliate landing pages, pagination, author archives, tag pages

options.name: confidence_threshold
options.label: Confidence threshold
options.description: How aggressively to filter uncertain URLs.

options.name: max_urls_per_prompt
options.label: Max URLs per prompt
options.description: Maximum number of URLs to send in a single LLM call. Larger batches use fewer API calls but may reduce accuracy.

options_defaults.keep_criteria: about pages, team, leadership, partnerships, integrations, products overview, services overview, awards, milestones, company news, investors, careers, pricing, contact, press releases, compliance, regulatory, case studies, interviews, expansion, acquisitions, funding, annual reports
options_defaults.drop_criteria: individual product pages, seasonal campaigns, single game pages, marketing landing pages, demo pages, how-to-play guides, promotional offers, individual blog posts about single products, holiday-themed content, affiliate landing pages, pagination, author archives, tag pages
